excerpt.as_written: اذ ابدا الفكر يشير عليك ان تدين او تفتشر مدبرك ففر من ذلك الفكر كما تفر من الزنا
translation: If the thought should start to occur to you that you should condemn or be in charge of your superior, flee from that thought just as from fornication

excerpt.as_written: منجل من اتمنت عليه سلمت وخلصت
translation: Because I have trusted him, I am delivered and saved

note: Many of the chapter division are translation of the Greek titles

excerpt.as_written: الميمر الاول عن التوبه التي تنبغا للكل في كل حين التي قوتها اخدوا المومنين المعموديه من روح القدس قبل فعل الوصايا
translation: First mimar on repentance which is necessary for all whenever baptized believers receive their nourishment from the Holy Spirit before fulfilling his commandments

excerpt.as_written: فعند ذلك تملك فيها النعمه بيسوع المسيح ربنا الذ في له السبح والكرامه دهر الداهرين امین
translation: Then, grace rules in it through Jesus Christ, our Lord, to whom be glory and honor forever and ever. Amen

contents.label: Mimar Three on the consultation of the mind with the soul (CPG 6098), f. 163r
Mimar Four on the Spiritual Law (CPG 6090), f. 166v
Mimar Six on those who think themselves fortunate in their works before God (CPG 6091), f. 192r
Mimar Seven on paradise and another on the spiritual law, f. 207v
Mimar Eight, Letter of Mark to Nicholaus, his disciple (CPG 6094), f. 212r

work_wit.alt_title: Diadochus of Photiki, One Hundred Chapters on Spiritual Perfection
work_wit.as_written: قول ذياذخس القديس كلام المنفعه الانفس علي الاسقيسس (f. 222v)

excerpt.as_written: حد الامانه التفكير بالله بلا طغا
translation: Limit of faith which considers God who has no limit

excerpt.as_written: من قبل الاهنا الجواد وملكنا يسوع المسيح لانه هوا له العدل وهو غلبتنا احباه وله غنا احسان ملكوته في الدهو ودهور الداهرین امین
translation: ...before our generous God and King, Jesus Christ, because justice is his and his love has prevailed and his is the fullness of the charity of his kingdom in eternity and forever. Amen

note: The work is divided with Greek numerals into a hundred (100) subsections

excerpt.as_written: Α ان اراد انسان ان يعمل بخور طيب الريح لبان صافي وصندل واظفار و ميعه بالسويه يردب مثل الطكس هذه هي الاربع الفضايل ان كانوا تامات متفقات ليس يسلم العقل
translation: If one wants to make a sweet-smelling perfume, he mixes pure frankincense, sandalwood, claws, and storax equally, just as for the liturgy. These are the four virtues; if they are perfected and consistent, the mind will not be handed over

excerpt.as_written: او ما اذا كنت قايم في صلاتك وانت فوق من كل فرح اخوحينيذ بالحقيقه اوجدت الصلاه
translation: Or whenever you stand in your prayers, above all else, brother, you are joy; at that time in truth you will find prayer

note: The text is divided with Greek numerals into one hundred and fifty-three (153) sections

work.desc_title: Dorotheus of Gaza, Diverse teaching XI

excerpt.as_written: جا مره القديس يعود بعض الاخوه فاصا به قد احم سبعه ايام واقام بعد ذلك اربعين يوما قبل ان ترجع اليه قوته
translation: Once the saint came to visit one of his brothers, and the brother said to him that he had had a fever for seven days, and after that, it had remained for forty days before his strength returned to him

excerpt.as_written: فاسل الله ان يسوينا ان نسمع ونحفظ ليلا يكون لنا زياده دينونه فيما نقرا ونسمع من قول الابهات
translation: So I ask God to make us equal that we listen and be mindful at night and that there be an increase of judgment in what we read and hear from the sayings of the fathers

note: The text is an excerpt, Doctrina XI, from a collection of Dorotheus’ work known as the Doctrinae diversae i-xvii (CPG 7352).

work.desc_title: Commentary on the parables of the holy Gospel of Matthew

excerpt.as_written: قال المسيح ان اذا رابتك عيند اليمين فانزعها والقيها عند فانه اخير لك ان تهلك بعض اوصالك ولا كل جسدك يلقا في جهنم
translation: Christ said that if your right hand offends you, remove it and throw it nearby, for it is better for you that some of your limbs perish, rather than all your body be cast into Gehenna

excerpt.as_written: التفسير الذين استوجرواهم عامه النصرانيه وعمل الكرم فهو عمل النواميس والوصايا والاولين الذين استوجروا في اول النهار وشرط لهم دينار فهم الذين امنو اول الناس
translation: Commentary: Those who hired them are Christians in general, and the work of the vineyard is the work of the laws and commandments. The first who were hired in the beginning of the day and contracted for a denarius are the first people who believed
